# Handson L10: Spark Structured Streaming + Machine Learning with MLlib

## Project Overview

This is a continuation of Handson L9. We previously built Tasks 1, 2, and 3 for a real-time ride-sharing analytics pipeline. In this assignment, we extend the pipeline by adding Machine Learning using Apache Spark MLlib.

---

## Project Structure

```
handson-l10/
├── models/
│   ├── fare_model/
│   └── fare_trend_model_v2/
├── data_generator.py
├── task4.py
├── task5.py
├── training-dataset.csv
└── README.md
```

---

## Prerequisites

```bash
pip3 install pyspark
pip3 install faker
```

---

## How to Run

```bash
# Terminal 1
python3 data_generator.py

# Terminal 2
python3 task4.py

# Terminal 3
python3 task5.py
```

---

## Task 4: Real-Time Fare Prediction Using MLlib Regression

### Approach

**Part 1 - Offline Model Training:**
- Load historical data from training-dataset.csv
- Cast distance_km and fare_amount to DoubleType
- Use VectorAssembler to combine distance_km into a features vector
- Train a LinearRegression model to learn the relationship between distance and fare
- Save the trained model to models/fare_model

**Part 2 - Real-Time Streaming Inference:**
- Connect to localhost:9999 and read incoming ride data
- Load the pre-trained model from disk
- Apply VectorAssembler to transform the streaming data
- Use the model to predict fare_amount for each incoming ride
- Calculate deviation = absolute difference between actual and predicted fare
- Print results to the console

### Output Columns

| Column | Description |
|---|---|
| trip_id | Unique ride ID |
| driver_id | Driver number |
| distance_km | Trip distance |
| fare_amount | Actual fare charged |
| predicted_fare | Models predicted fare |
| deviation | Difference between actual and predicted |

### Task 4 Output Screenshot

![Task 4 Output](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Task 5: Time-Based Fare Trend Prediction

### Approach

**Part 1 - Offline Model Training:**
- Load historical data from training-dataset.csv
- Aggregate data into 5-minute time windows and calculate avg_fare
- Extract hour_of_day and minute_of_hour features from window start time
- Train a LinearRegression model to predict average fare based on time
- Save the trained model to models/fare_trend_model_v2

**Part 2 - Real-Time Streaming Inference:**
- Connect to localhost:9999 and read incoming ride data
- Apply 5-minute sliding window aggregation sliding every 1 minute
- Apply same feature engineering to extract hour_of_day and minute_of_hour
- Load the pre-trained model and apply it to the windowed streaming data
- Print window_start, window_end, avg_fare, and predicted_next_avg_fare

### Output Columns

| Column | Description |
|---|---|
| window_start | Start time of the 5-minute window |
| window_end | End time of the 5-minute window |
| avg_fare | Actual average fare in this window |
| predicted_next_avg_fare | Predicted average fare |

### Task 5 Output Screenshot 1

![Task 5 Output 1](data:image/png;base64,)

### Task 5 Output Screenshot 2

## Results Summary

### Task 4 Results
The LinearRegression model successfully learned the relationship between distance_km and fare_amount. High deviation values indicate rides where the actual fare was significantly different from what the model expected.

### Task 5 Results
The model successfully predicted average fare trends based on time of day using 5-minute windowed aggregation and time-based feature engineering.

---
